content.problemStatement:
A sequence of integers (a1,a2,…,aka_1, a_2, \ldots, a_ka1​,a2​,…,ak​) is said to be UpDown, if these inequalities hold true:


a1≤a2a_1 \le a_2a1​≤a2​
a2≥a3a_2 \ge a_3a2​≥a3​
a3≤a4a_3 \le a_4a3​≤a4​
and so on.
That is, every even-indexed element should be at least as large as its adjacent elements. And every odd-indexed element should be at most as large as its adjacent elements. Formally, a2i≥a2i+1a_{2i} \ge a_{2i+1}a2i​≥a2i+1​ and a2i+1≤a2i+2a_{2i+1} \le a_{2i+2}a2i+1​≤a2i+2​, for all valid positions.

A subsegment is a consecutive portion of a sequence. That is, a subsegment of (b1,b2,…,bkb_1, b_2, \ldots, b_kb1​,b2​,…,bk​)  will be of the form (bi,bi+1,…,bjb_i, b_{i+1}, \ldots, b_jbi​,bi+1​,…,bj​), for some iii and jjj.

You are given a sequence (s1,s2,…,sns_1, s_2, \ldots, s_ns1​,s2​,…,sn​). You can insert at most one integer anywhere in this sequence. It could be any integer. After inserting an integer (or choosing not to), suppose you have the new sequence (t1,t2,…,tmt_1, t_2, \ldots, t_mt1​,t2​,…,tm​). Note that mmm will either be nnn+1 or nnn. You want to maximize the length of the longest subsegment of (t1,t2,…,tmt_1, t_2, \ldots, t_mt1​,t2​,…,tm​) which is UpDown, and output the length of that.

###Input


The first line contains a single integer, TTT, which is the number of testcases. The description of each testcase follows.
The first line of every testcase contains a single integer, nnn, which is the number of integers in the original sequence.
The second line contains nnn integers: s1,s2,…,sns_1, s_2, \ldots, s_ns1​,s2​,…,sn​, which forms the original sequence.
###Output
For each testcase output a single line containing one integer, which should be the length of the longest UpDown subsegment that you can get after inserting at most one integer.

###Constraints


1≤T≤21 \le T \le 21≤T≤2
1≤n≤1061 \le n \le 10^61≤n≤106
1≤si≤1091 \le s_i \le 10^91≤si​≤109
###Subtasks

**Subtask #1 (20 points):** 1≤n≤1001 \le n \le 1001≤n≤100

**Subtask #2 (10 points):** 1≤n≤100001 \le n \le 100001≤n≤10000
content.inputFormat: The first line contains a single integer, T, which is the number of testcases. The description of each testcase follows. The first line of every testcase contains a single integer, n, which is the number of integers in the original sequence. The second line contains n integers: s1, s2, …, sn, which forms the original sequence.
content.outputFormat: For each testcase output a single line containing one integer, which should be the length of the longest UpDown subsegment that you can get after inserting at most one integer.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, the longest UpDown subsegment is (1, 10, 3, 20, 5, 25, 24) with a length of 7. For the second test case, the longest UpDown subsegment is (3, 3, 2, 4, 1, 4) with a length of 6.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use dynamic programming to find the longest UpDown subsegment. We will maintain two arrays, one for the longest UpDown subsegment ending at each position with an even index and another for the longest UpDown subsegment ending at each position with an odd index. We will then update these arrays based on the given conditions for UpDown sequences.
solution.timeComplexity: O(n) — where n is the number of integers in the sequence. We are scanning the sequence once to fill up the dynamic programming arrays.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n) — for the dynamic programming arrays.
